body:
## What does this PR do?

Redesigns the flow-builder testing experience end-to-end. The bottom test panel that used to permanently take half the right sidebar is replaced with two switchable layouts (a slide-up drawer or a side-by-side split), test input/output gains a Friendly View / Raw JSON toggle so non-developers can scan results, and the Data Selector mentions popover gets a tighter, more legible visual pass with reliable hover-to-insert and search that surfaces deep value matches.

The change is purely UX-layer — no API, schema, or testing-pipeline changes — and respects all existing edition gates, the readonly run-review mode, and manual triggers.

### Explain How the Feature Works

**Testing panel — two views, one toggle.** The default is a slide-up drawer hidden until the user clicks the sticky outline `Test Step` CTA at the bottom of the form (single click both opens the drawer and fires the test for piece actions). The alternative is a side-by-side split — selectable from the layout-toggle icon in the test panel header. The right sidebar auto-widens to ~55% (min 700px) in split mode and reverts to ~25% in drawer mode. The choice persists per user via `localStorage`. The drawer's grip handle is draggable (25–95% height); a click without movement closes it.

**Single shared top header.** The piece icon, editable step name, and prev/next-step navigation now live once in the SidebarHeader. Below it, a compact `SETTINGS` sub-header carries the piece version + update-piece-version control. The previous duplicate `StepInfo` row is gone.

**New unified test panel header.** Status (`Tested` / `Testing…` / `Failed`) + date on the left; retest, copy, download, and the layout toggle on the right. Copy and download follow whichever Input/Output/Logs tab is currently selected, and tooltips reflect it ("Copy Input", "Download Output").

**Friendly View / Raw JSON.** Each Input/Output/Logs tab now has an inner Friendly/Raw segmented control. Friendly walks the sample data and renders a key-value tree with expandable groups, "N items" counts for arrays, "Show more" for long strings, and primary-coloured emails/URLs. Raw keeps the existing `react-json-view` tree (with optional `hideHeader` to merge into the new chrome).

**Improved testing states.** The first-time empty state is a centered icon + helpful copy + primary "Test Step" button instead of a tiny outlined button. The loading state is a single muted spinner with a ghost Cancel — replacing the old skeleton + detached cancel row.

**Data Selector polish.**
- Tighter rows (32px / 40px for step root), smaller piece icon (xs), label capped at 40% so values keep their space.
- Leaves render `key : value` with the value styled as a primary mention, truncated with a tooltip.
- Arrays show an "N items" muted hint next to the chevron.
- Hover-revealed `Insert` button on every insertable leaf (clicking the row still inserts as a fallback).
- Search no longer caps auto-expand at depth 1 — value matches at any depth become visible without manual unfolding.
- The "needs to be tested" placeholder is now a single inline notice strip with a small `Go to trigger` / `Go to step` link, not a centered card.

### Relevant User Scenarios

- A user configuring a piece action wants to run a quick test and see the result inline without losing the full settings form — drawer mode, click the CTA, the drawer slides up with the result.
- A power user iterating on a complex action wants both panels visible at once — split mode, the sidebar widens and the test panel sits to the right of the form.
- A non-developer reviewing test output wants human-readable fields, not a JSON tree — Friendly View on Output.
- A user mapping fields from a previous step wants to find a specific email value in a sample — type "codecademy" in the data selector; the matching leaf is auto-expanded into view, hover reveals Insert.
- Reviewing a past run from the Runs sidebar — drawer auto-opens with the run's input/output, retest hidden, copy/download act on the active tab.

Fixes # (issue)
title: feat(builder): redesign testing UX with drawer/split panels, Friendly View output, and refreshed data selector

body:
## What does this PR do?

Two improvements to the **Automations** page:

**1. Create flows & tables directly inside a folder.**
Adds a hover **+** button on every folder row in the Automations table. Clicking it opens a folder-scoped dropdown — **New Flow**, **New Table**, **Import Flow**, **Import Table** — that creates the item directly inside that folder. For Import flows/tables the target folder also auto-expands so the user can see the destination behind the dialog. This removes the create-at-root-then-Move-To workaround that customers (especially embed customers with many folders) have been complaining about.

**2. Brand-aware empty state.**
The two "Get started" cards on the empty Automations page no longer use hardcoded purple/emerald. The **Build a Flow** card now uses the platform's primary brand colour (via `bg-primary-100` / `text-primary`), and the **Create a Table** card uses a neutral muted treatment so the two cards stay visually distinct. Embed and Cloud customers with custom branding now see their own colour on the first-run screen.

The page-header **Create New** button is unchanged for users who want items at the root.

### Explain How the Feature Works

- **CreateNewMenu** is a new shared component reused by both the page-header **Create New** button (`scope="root"`) and the new folder-row hover button (`scope="folder"`). The scope prop controls which entries appear (root scope shows New Folder + Start from Template; folder scope shows Import Flow + Import Table). Each instance owns its own open state, so a creation in one folder's menu cannot force-open the menus on other folders.
- **useAutomationsMutations** now exposes folder-aware `createFlow(folderId?)` and `createTable(name, folderId?)`. Both delegate to `flowsApi.create({ folderId })` and a new `tableHooks.createTableWithDefaults({ name, folderId, projectId })` helper that centralises the multi-step "table + default field + default record" creation.
- **Auto-expand on import** is handled in `automations/index.tsx`: for `import-flow` / `import-table`, the page calls `toggleFolder(folderId)` if the folder isn't already expanded, so the user sees the import destination behind the dialog. For `flow` / `table`, the create mutation navigates straight to the builder, so no expand is needed (and would be invisible).
- **ImportTableDialog** and `importTablesFromTemplates` now accept an optional `folderId` so imports targeted from the hover button land in the right folder.
- **Embed flags** are respected — `hideTables`, `hideFolders`, and `hideExportAndImportFlow` hide the corresponding entries in both the header and the hover menu.
- **Permissions** use the existing `PermissionNeededTooltip` pattern. A user without `Permission.WRITE_FLOW` / `Permission.WRITE_TABLE` sees disabled menu items with the tooltip explanation.
- **Empty-state colours** flow through the existing `ThemeProvider` CSS variables (`--primary`, `--primary-100`, `--primary-300`) that are already injected from `branding.colors.primary`. No new theme plumbing required.

### Relevant User Scenarios

**Power users with many folders** can now create flows/tables exactly where they want them in one click instead of two.

**Embed customers** stop seeing Activepieces' default purple on their empty Automations page — they get their own brand colour, matching the rest of the white-labelled experience they've configured. This was the customer complaint that prompted the empty-state half of this PR.

Customer discussions:
- https://activepieces.slack.com/archives/C07GT9LCCMT/p1777016083829029
- https://activepieces.slack.com/archives/C08RZD9JB5X/p1776877104244439

### Edition behaviour

| Edition | + button on folder row | Empty-state branding |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Community (self-hosted) | Yes | Default purple |
| Enterprise (self-hosted) | Yes | Branded if Custom Appearance is enabled, else default |
| Cloud | Yes | Always branded |
| Embed | Yes — Tables / Import items hide automatically when the embed config hides them | Always branded (inherits platform) |

No backend, schema, or shared-package changes — purely `packages/web`.

Fixes # (issue)
title: feat(web): create flows/tables inside folders + branded empty state

## What does this PR do?

Cleans up the Baserow piece's French translation, resyncs `translation.json` with the actual source code strings, fixes a few brand-naming issues, and reorders the action list to a more logical flow. Bumps piece to **0.9.1** (patch).

### Changes

**1. French i18n — fix mistranslations + terminology**

The French file had been polluted by an automated translation pass that produced several wrong or absurd strings:

| Key | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `Table` | Tableau | **Table** |
| `Sum` | Sum (untranslated) | Somme |
| `Max` | Max. (extra period) | Max |
| `Median` | Moyenne (duplicate of Average!) | Médiane |
| `Variance` | Variante (wrong word) | Variance |
| `GET` / `POST` / `PUT` / `DELETE` / `HEAD` | OBTENIR / POSTER / EFFACER / SUPPRIMER / TÊTE | GET / POST / PUT / DELETE / HEAD |
| `Page Size` | Nombre d'élément (singular) | Lignes par page |
| `Body Type` | Body Type (untranslated) | Type de corps |
| `Standard deviation` | Écart type | Écart-type |

The **terminology rule** applied throughout:

- **Table** = the abstract data structure (a database concept) → translates to **"Table"** in French.
- **Tableau** = the French word for the **grid view** (one *presentation* of a table among others). The same table can be presented as a `tableau` (grid), `galerie` (gallery), `calendrier` (calendar), `kanban`, `formulaire` (form), etc.

Translating "Table" as "Tableau" conflates the data structure with one of its possible views, which is exactly the confusion auto-translators introduce.

**2. Resync `translation.json` with current source strings**

Several action/trigger descriptions had been rewritten in source code without updating the i18n keys, leaving orphan entries in both `translation.json` and `fr.json`:

- Auth description rewritten ("Connect to Baserow using either…") — old "Choose how you want to authenticate…" key was orphan
- Aggregate Field description ("…in a grid view") — old "…in a table" key was orphan
- List Rows: typo `+ sing for ascending` (instead of `sign`) → French had auto-translated as **"chanter"** (to sing) 🤡
- Trigger display names: `Row Created → New Row`, `Row Event → Any Row Change`, `Rows Created (Batch) → New Rows (Batch)` — translation keys updated
- Aggregation type description: now "Sum, Average, Min, Max, Median, Std Dev, and Variance only work with number fields"

Added missing keys for the auth surface (`Database Token (recommended)`, the 🔑/👤 markdown sections, all field descriptions), the `View` label and `Select a table first.` placeholder of the Aggregate Field action, and the new webhook setup markdowns (4 event-label variants).

**3. Reorder actions in `index.ts`**

The previous order mixed CRUD, advanced ops, and batches arbitrarily. New logical flow:

1. CRUD on a single row: Create → Get → List → Find → Update → Upsert → Delete → Clean
2. Batch: Batch Create → Batch Update → Batch Delete
3. Advanced: Aggregate Field → Upload File
4. Custom API Call (always last)

**4. Bump piece version**

`0.9.0 → 0.9.1` (patch — i18n + non-functional reordering, no breaking changes).

### Explain How the Feature Works

The piece's i18n bundle (`translation.json` + per-locale files like `fr.json`) is loaded by Activepieces at runtime to localize action/trigger labels, descriptions, dropdowns, and markdown blocks visible in the flow builder. Activepieces matches each English source string against the locale file's keys.

When source strings drift from the locale keys (e.g. a description gets rewritten in code without updating the JSON), the locale lookup misses and the UI falls back to English — that's why the auth screen shown to French users was a mix of EN and FR strings. This PR realigns all keys with the actual source strings and provides correct French translations.

### Relevant User Scenarios

- French-speaking users on Activepieces Cloud / Enterprise see a fully localized Baserow piece (auth dialog, every action's params, dropdowns, helper texts, webhook setup instructions).
- The Aggregate Field action's aggregation dropdown now reads cleanly: Somme / Moyenne / Min / Max / Nombre (non vides) / Médiane / Écart-type / Variance — instead of mixing English fragments and a duplicate "Moyenne" for Median.
- Users browsing the Baserow piece in the action picker see the actions in a logical order matching their workflow (create something → query it → modify it → batch-process → aggregate → upload files), regardless of the cloud's search ranking.

### Notes for reviewers

- No source-code logic changes — only `index.ts` array reorder, version bump, and i18n JSON files.
- The French `Table` (data structure) ↔ `Tableau` (grid presentation) distinction is critical for any database-style piece (Baserow, Airtable, NocoDB, SeaTable). Same review pattern should apply to those pieces.
- ⚠️ Risk: if the auto-translation tool ever runs a global "regenerate all locales" pass, these manual fixes may be overwritten. No locking mechanism currently exists in this repo.
title: fix(baserow): clean French i18n, sync translation keys, reorder actions

## What does this PR do?

Adds **Variables** — encrypted project-scoped secret values (API keys, tokens, opaque strings) that users create once and reference inside any flow input via a mention pill:

```
{{variables['STRIPE_API_KEY']}}
```

The value is stored AES-encrypted at rest, decrypted only by the engine at flow execution (via a worker endpoint) or by an authorized user via the explicit reveal action. Every reveal is audit-logged.

> History: this branch was originally `credentials-manager` and piggy-backed on `app_connection` via a `kind` discriminator and nullable `pieceName`. After team review that coupling was scrapped in favour of a clean, dedicated module — the `app_connection` table is back to its pre-branch shape and Variables live in their own table, service, controller, permissions, and audit events.

### Architecture

**New `variable` table** (`packages/server/api/src/app/variable/`)
- Columns: `id, created, updated, name, projectId, platformId, ownerId, value (jsonb, encrypted), metadata`
- Unique index on `(projectId, name)`; SET NULL FK to `user`
- Migration: `1793000000000-AddVariableTable.ts`

**Routes**
| Method | Path | Auth | Permission |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/v1/variables` | USER + SERVICE | `WRITE_VARIABLE` (upsert by name; fires `VARIABLE_UPSERTED`) |
| `GET` | `/v1/variables` | USER + SERVICE | `READ_VARIABLE` (paginated; filters by `name` substring) |
| `GET` | `/v1/variables/owners` | USER + SERVICE | `READ_VARIABLE` (distinct project members owning at least one variable) |
| `POST` | `/v1/variables/:id/reveal` | USER only | `WRITE_VARIABLE` (fires `VARIABLE_VALUE_REVEALED`) |
| `DELETE` | `/v1/variables/:id` | USER + SERVICE | `WRITE_VARIABLE` (fires `VARIABLE_DELETED`) |
| `GET` | `/v1/worker/variables/:name` | ENGINE only | (engine principal carries projectId) |

**Engine** — `packages/server/engine/src/lib/piece-context/variable-resolver.ts` mirrors the connection resolver. `props-resolver` adds a `variables` branch alongside `connections`; censored input renders `**REDACTED**`.

**Permissions** — new `READ_VARIABLE` / `WRITE_VARIABLE` added to the project ACL. ADMIN + EDITOR get write; VIEWER reads only. The reveal endpoint is USER-principal only.

**Audit events** — `VARIABLE_UPSERTED`, `VARIABLE_DELETED`, `VARIABLE_VALUE_REVEALED`. Each carries `{ variable: { id, name } }`.

### Web UI

- **`/variables` page** with paginated table, owner filter, search by name, bulk delete, per-row dropdown (Edit / Copy reference / Copy value / Delete).
  - **Name** · **Owner** · **Used in** (flow count, plural-aware) · **Last updated** · **Actions**.
- **Variable dialog** for create / rotate value, with the eye toggle.
- **Builder data-selector tab** to insert `{{variables['NAME']}}` mentions.
- **Mention chip** renders the lucide `Variable` icon plus `Variable · <name>`.
- Audit-log rows and event-destination labels learn the three new event types.

### Permission summary (UI)

| Role | List page | New / Edit / Rotate / Delete | Copy reference | Copy value (reveal) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ADMIN | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| EDITOR | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| VIEWER | ✓ | – | ✓ | – |

Copy reference is gated only by `READ_VARIABLE` — it writes the public mention syntax to the clipboard, never the plaintext.

### Manual cleanup for the preview env

The earlier `1791000000001-RelaxAppConnectionPieceFields` migration ran on the preview DB but is now deleted. Run this once against the preview env to restore the original `app_connection` schema:

```sql
DELETE FROM "app_connection" WHERE "pieceName" IS NULL;
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS "idx_app_connection_piece_name";
ALTER TABLE "app_connection" ALTER COLUMN "pieceName" SET NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE "app_connection" ALTER COLUMN "pieceVersion" SET NOT NULL;
DELETE FROM "migrations" WHERE "name" = 'RelaxAppConnectionPieceFields1791000000001';
```

Fresh installs (and prod once this lands) are unaffected — the migration registry never sees it.

## Test plan

- [ ] **CRUD** — Create `STRIPE`, edit/rotate the value, delete it from `/variables`. Confirm rows appear/disappear and the `variable` table reflects them.
- [ ] **Reveal + audit** — Copy a variable's value; confirm the plaintext matches. A `VARIABLE_VALUE_REVEALED` row appears at `/platform/security/audit-logs`. Repeat for `VARIABLE_UPSERTED` and `VARIABLE_DELETED`.
- [ ] **Mention rendering** — Drop a variable into an HTTP step's Authorization header via the data-selector. The pill renders `Variable · STRIPE`; saved value is `{{variables['STRIPE']}}`.
- [ ] **Flow execution** — Run a flow that references a variable. The engine resolves it via `/v1/worker/variables/:name` and the external service receives the correct value.
- [ ] **Used-in column** — Reference the same variable in 3 flows; the "Used in" cell shows `3 flows` and updates to `No flows` after removing the references.
- [ ] **Owner filter** — Create variables as two different project members; the owner filter dropdown shows both, multi-select narrows the table.
- [ ] **Permission gating** — As a VIEWER, list works but New / Edit / Rotate / Copy value / Delete are disabled. As an EDITOR, all actions work. `POST /:id/reveal` returns 403 for VIEWER.
- [ ] **Connections page unchanged** — Connections list, replace, reconnect, rename all still pass. No `kind` query param, no `pieceName IS NULL` rows.
- [ ] **MCP** — `ap_list_connections` returns piece connections only.
- [ ] **Docs** — `.agents/features/variables.md` exists; credentials sections removed from `app-connections.md`, `global-connections.md`, `mcp.md`.

## Summary
Adds an **SSO Domain** field on the platform with DNS-based ownership verification, surfaced as a new item under Platform Settings → SSO. Non-breaking — admins can opt in to set & verify a domain, but existing SAML setups continue to work unchanged.

- New `ssoDomain` (varchar, unique) and `ssoDomainVerification` (jsonb) columns on `platform`
- Admin can save a domain → server issues a TXT record (`_activepieces-verify.<domain>`) and stores `{ status: PENDING_VERIFICATION, record }`
- Admin clicks **Verify DNS** → server resolves the TXT record and flips status to `VERIFIED` if it matches
- New endpoints: `POST /v1/authn/saml/sso-domain`, `POST /v1/authn/saml/sso-domain/verify` (both `platformAdminOnly` + `ssoEnabled` plan gate)
- New SSO page item with verification status badge + dialog matching the embed-page DNS verification UX

## Why non-breaking
- Login / SAML discovery flow is untouched. The verified domain isn't read anywhere yet — this PR only collects and verifies it.
- Existing platforms with SAML already configured continue to work; they can verify their domain at their own pace.
- A follow-up PR will wire the verified domain into SAML discovery so customers have time to set up DNS first.

## Test plan
- [ ] Run the new migration (or apply manually): adds nullable \`ssoDomain\` + \`ssoDomainVerification\` columns and a unique partial index.
- [ ] Open Platform Settings → SSO, see the new **SSO Domain** item.

## What does this PR do?

Fixes the Snowflake OAuth connection flow, which was failing for every customer with `The requested scope is invalid` before they could even authenticate.

The piece was sending `session:role-any refresh_token` as the OAuth scope. Per Snowflake's [custom OAuth docs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/oauth-custom), `session:role-any` is only valid for **External OAuth** integrations (where another IdP like Okta or Azure issues the token). For the **custom client integration** that the piece's setup SQL creates (`OAUTH_CLIENT = CUSTOM`), the only accepted scope values are `refresh_token` and `session:role:<ROLE_NAME>`. Snowflake therefore rejected the request at `/oauth/authorize` before the user was shown the sign-in screen.

This PR:

- Drops `session:role-any` from the requested OAuth scope, leaving `refresh_token`.
- Adds `OAUTH_USE_SECONDARY_ROLES = IMPLICIT` to the setup SQL so the **Default Role** field still works for switching to any role granted to the user after the token is issued.
- Bumps the piece version `0.3.1 → 0.3.2`.


### Explain How the Feature Works

After the change, the OAuth flow looks like this:

1. The user runs the updated `CREATE SECURITY INTEGRATION` SQL in Snowflake (now including `OAUTH_USE_SECONDARY_ROLES = IMPLICIT`).
2. The piece redirects the browser to `https://<account>.snowflakecomputing.com/oauth/authorize?…&scope=refresh_token`.
3. Snowflake accepts the scope, the user signs in, and the token is issued for the user's default Snowflake role.
4. When a query runs, `snowflake.createConnection({ role })` switches the session to the role configured in the **Default Role** field. `OAUTH_USE_SECONDARY_ROLES = IMPLICIT` is what lets the token use any role granted to the user, so the Default Role field is no longer silently ignored.

Verified end-to-end against a fresh Snowflake trial account: the previous `scope=session:role-any+refresh_token` URL reproduces the customer's "The requested scope is invalid" error, and the patched `scope=refresh_token` URL passes through to the sign-in screen and issues a working token.

<!-- [Insert the video link here] -->


### Relevant User Scenarios

- **Any customer setting up a new Snowflake OAuth connection.** The integration was effectively broken for new connections — the authorize step rejected the request before authentication, so no one could complete OAuth without falling back to the username/password or key-pair auth path.
- **Customers who want to use a non-default Snowflake role at query time.** The added `OAUTH_USE_SECONDARY_ROLES = IMPLICIT` keeps the **Default Role** field on the connection form functional after the scope change; without it, the OAuth token would be bound to the user's default role and the field would be silently ignored.

<!-- [Insert Pylon tickets or community posts here if possible] -->


Fixes # (issue)
title: fix(snowflake): drop invalid session:role-any scope from OAuth flow

body:
## What does this PR do?

<!-- We need a clear description of what the PR does, as this will be used for the marketing team to generate the release notes. -->

Hardens the Microsoft Teams piece against Microsoft Graph throttling and the engine's 60s sandbox timeout. Customers with large Teams accounts (hundreds of chats, or company-wide teams with thousands of members) were intermittently seeing `Unexpected error, please retry` on the **Chat ID** dropdown in **Send Chat Message**, and the same failure mode was latent on every other dynamic dropdown in the piece.

This PR introduces a `paginateGraphList` helper that combines retry-with-backoff, graceful error handling, and a per-dropdown item cap, then applies it to all five dynamic dropdowns. It also wraps every paginated Graph call in the four polling triggers and the **Send Channel Message** action with the same retry helper so transient throttling no longer fails entire flow runs.

### Explain How the Feature Works

<!-- Adding a video demonstration is optional but encourged! It helps reviewers / marketing team understand your implementation better. -->
<!-- [Insert the video link here] -->

**Root cause.** The Chat ID dropdown loaded `/chats?$expand=members` and walked every page sequentially with no `$top`, no retry, no cap, and no error handling. For an account with ~700 chats this resulted in ~35 round-trips to a heavy `$expand=members` endpoint that Graph aggressively throttles. Total walltime exceeded the 60s `TRIGGER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` cap on dropdown property execution. The worker killed the sandbox and returned an empty `response: {}`, the API forwarded it as-is, the frontend dropdown component couldn't render the malformed response, and the React error boundary surfaced `Unexpected error, please retry`.

**The fix has four pillars, all encapsulated in `paginateGraphList`:**

1. **Retry on transient errors** — wraps every Graph request in `withGraphRetry`, which retries 429 / 503 / 504 / 5xx with exponential backoff and honors `Retry-After`.
2. **Graceful failure** — wraps the entire pagination loop in `tryCatch`. If everything fails the dropdown returns a disabled state with a friendly placeholder. If a partial load succeeds before an error, those items are returned.
3. **Explicit page size where supported** — `/chats` supports `$top` (max 50) so the helper now requests 50 per page instead of Graph's default 20. This alone cuts the original 35 round-trips to ~10. Other endpoints (`/me/joinedTeams`, `/teams/{id}/channels`, `/teams/{id}/members`) don't support `$top` and rely on Graph defaults.
4. **Per-dropdown item cap of 500** — guarantees the load completes inside the sandbox budget regardless of account size. The 500 most recently active chats / first 500 channels / first 500 members are returned.

**Other resilience improvements:**

- Wrapped the four polling triggers (`new-chat`, `new-chat-message`, `new-channel`, `new-channel-message`) and the `Send Channel Message` action's mention-resolution pagination in `withGraphRetry`. A single 429 mid-poll no longer fails the entire trigger run.
- Removed `409` from the retry list — it's not a safe blanket retry on Graph delta endpoints, where it signals that the stored deltalink is stale and the sync state must restart.
- Removed two stray `console.log` debug statements from the New Chat trigger.

**Backward compatibility.** Verified end-to-end:

- All dropdown property names, value contracts (string IDs), label formats, and `displayName`/`description`/`required`/`refreshers` configurations are unchanged.
- Existing flows that already reference a chat / team / channel / member outside the new 500-item cap continue to resolve at runtime — the saved value is a string ID, the action passes it directly to Graph, and no dropdown loading happens at execution time.
- All retried errors fail in the same final state as before (just with up to ~7s of backoff first).

### Relevant User Scenarios

<!-- List specific use cases where this feature would be valuable. -->
<!-- [Insert Pylon tickets or community posts here if possible] -->

- **Enterprise customers with large Teams tenants** — company-wide teams with thousands of members or accounts with hundreds of active chats can now configure **Send Chat Message**, **Create Chat & Send Message**, and **Send Channel Message** without the Chat ID / Team / Channel / Member dropdowns timing out.
- **Customers hitting Microsoft Graph throttling** — transient 429s during dropdown loads or trigger polls are now retried transparently with `Retry-After`-aware backoff instead of failing.
- **Customers running Teams triggers in production** — `New Chat`, `New Chat Message`, `New Channel`, `New Channel Message` no longer skip a poll cycle on a single transient Graph hiccup.
- **Customers seeing "Unexpected error, please retry" on dynamic dropdowns** — failures now degrade to a disabled-with-placeholder state ("Couldn't load X — please retry") instead of triggering the React error boundary.

Reproduction was verified locally by stubbing the Graph client to return 700 paginated chats with a 3s per-page delay, which reliably reproduces the 60s sandbox timeout against the broken code and loads cleanly against the fix.

Fixes # (issue)
title: fix(microsoft-teams): make dropdown loaders resilient to throttling a…

body:
## Summary
Adds server-side chat compaction that automatically summarizes older conversation messages when the context window is running out of space. This prevents provider 400 errors on long conversations and reduces token costs.

## How It Works

### Problem
The chat feature stores the entire conversation history in a JSONB `messages` column and sends **all messages** to the LLM on every request. When conversations grow beyond the model's context window, the provider returns a 400 error and the conversation breaks with no recovery.

### Solution — Hybrid Compaction (Summary + Recent Window)
Before each `streamText()` call, the server estimates the token count and checks if it exceeds 70% of the provider's context limit. When triggered:

1. **Summarize** — Calls `generateText()` with a structured prompt that preserves user decisions, entity names, tool call results, and the current task
2. **Split** — Keeps recent messages verbatim (the "recent window") and replaces older messages with the summary
3. **Store** — Saves the summary in new `summary` and `summarizedUpToIndex` columns; the full `messages` array is preserved for UI rendering
4. **Send** — Passes `[summary_message, ...recent_messages]` to the LLM instead of the full history

The compaction is **fully transparent** — the user sees the complete message history in the UI, and the model retains context about earlier messages via the summary.

### Key Design Decisions
| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Trigger | Lazy — only at 70% of provider context limit |
| Token estimation | Character heuristic (~4 chars/token) |
| Context limits | Per-provider map (OpenAI: 1M, Anthropic: 200K, Google: 1M, etc.) with 128K default |
| Summarization model | Same model as the conversation |
| Storage | Separate `summary` + `summarizedUpToIndex` columns; `messages` untouched |
| Tool call safety | Respects assistant→tool message pairs to avoid orphaned `tool_result` errors |
| Overflow fallback | Progressively shrinks recent window; throws `CHAT_CONTEXT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` if even minimal payload is too large |

## Changes

### New Files
- `packages/server/api/src/app/chat/chat-compaction.ts` — Compaction module (estimateTokenCount, shouldCompact, compactMessages, buildCompactedPayload)
- `packages/server/api/src/app/database/migration/postgres/1786000000000-AddChatCompactionColumns.ts` — Migration adding `summary` and `summarizedUpToIndex` columns
- `packages/server/api/test/unit/app/chat/chat-compaction.test.ts` — 13 unit tests

### Modified Files
- `chat-service.ts` — Integrated compaction into `sendMessage()` via `resolveCompactionState` helper
- `chat-conversation-entity.ts` — Added `summary` (String, nullable) and `summarizedUpToIndex` (Number, nullable) columns
- `ai-providers/index.ts` — Added `PROVIDER_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS` map and `aiProviderUtils.getMaxContextTokens()`
- `activepieces-error.ts` — Added `CHAT_CONTEXT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` error code
- `error-handler.ts` — Mapped new error code to 400
- `chat/index.ts` (shared) — Added fields to `ChatConversation` schema
- `shared/package.json` — Bumped to 0.68.6

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests for `estimateTokenCount`, `shouldCompact`, `buildCompactedPayload` including tool boundary edge cases (13 tests)
- [x] End-to-end manual test: compaction triggered on real conversation, summary stored in DB, model retained context from summarized messages
- [x] Verified tool call boundary handling prevents Anthropic `tool_result` pairing errors
- [ ] Verify on OpenAI and Google providers
- [ ] Verify re-compaction (extending an existing summary with more messages)
- [x] `npm run lint-dev` passes with 0 errors
- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly

body:
## Summary

Customer (Microsoft Entra / Azure AD) hit two errors configuring SAML SSO:

1. `INVALID_SAML_RESPONSE — "should contain firstName, lastName, email fields"` — even when the customer had those fields, because Entra sends `http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/emailaddress` etc., and our validator only looked up the literal keys `email` / `firstName` / `lastName`.
2. `500 Internal Server Error — Cannot read properties of null (reading 'map')` — once the customer added extra Entra claims (tenantid, objectidentifier, role, authnmethodsreferences). The error originates inside `samlify`'s attribute aggregation when extracted values are `null` and bubbled up as a generic 500 with no diagnostic.

This PR fixes both:

- **Out-of-the-box Entra / AD-FS support** — resolve `email` / `firstName` / `lastName` from a candidate list that includes simple names, lowercase variants, and the standard `http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/...` URIs.
- **Optional `attributeMapping`** added to `SAMLAuthnProviderConfig` for IdPs that use custom names (e.g. `urn:oid:0.9.2342.19200300.100.1.3`). Admin overrides win, defaults are the fallback.
- **`tryCatch` around `parseLoginResponse`** so samlify internals failing on null-valued claims surface as `INVALID_SAML_RESPONSE` (4xx) instead of 500.

### Summary

Adds a shared `loggerRedact` config in `@activepieces/server-utils` and wires it into every production pino instance (api root logger + 4 transport providers + worker). Sensitive fields in log payloads are replaced with `[REDACTED]` so credentials don't leak to stdout, files, or third-party log sinks (Logtail/Better Stack, Hyperdx, Loki, OTel).

- Censor: `[REDACTED]` (object shape preserved, `remove: false`)

### Notes

This ruleset is a **draft** — it will be tuned as we observe real log output and discover new shapes that need redaction (or over-redactions that hurt debuggability). The path list is a living document, not a final spec.

body:
## What does this PR do?

Expands the **ServiceNow** piece from 6 actions / 2 triggers to **17 actions / 3 triggers**, adding coverage for the Knowledge Management API, Service Catalog API, Email API, Aggregate API, and the journal-field/comments table — closing all the major gaps versus Zapier, Make, n8n, and Workato. Also replaces the hand-rolled custom API call with the shared `createCustomApiCallAction` helper used by Airtable, Intercom, and Freshservice.

### Explain How the Feature Works

The piece is built on ServiceNow's REST APIs (Yokohama bundle) and works against any standard or custom table. Authentication is unchanged — basic auth against an instance URL.

**New actions:**

| Action | ServiceNow endpoint | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Delete Record | `DELETE /api/now/table/{table}/{sys_id}` | Remove a record from any table |
| Add Comment or Work Note | `PATCH /api/now/table/{table}/{sys_id}` (`comments` / `work_notes`) | Append a customer-visible comment or internal work note |
| Resolve or Close Incident | `PATCH /api/now/table/incident/{sys_id}` (state 6 / 7 + `close_code` + `close_notes`) | Convenience action over `update_record` for the most common ITSM workflow step |
| Delete Attachment | `DELETE /api/now/attachment/{sys_id}` | Complete the attachment lifecycle |
| Get Catalog Item | `GET /api/sn_sc/servicecatalog/items/{sys_id}` | Fetch a catalog item with its variable definitions |
| Submit Catalog Request | `POST /api/sn_sc/servicecatalog/items/{sys_id}/order_now` | Order a catalog item programmatically |
| Search Knowledge Articles | `GET /api/sn_km_api/knowledge/articles` | Free-text KB search |
| Get Knowledge Article | `GET /api/sn_km_api/knowledge/articles/{sys_id}` | Fetch full article content |
| Send Email | `POST /api/now/v1/email` | Send notifications through ServiceNow's email engine |
| Count Records | `GET /api/now/stats/{table}` (`sysparm_count=true`) | Aggregate API — count rows matching a query |
| Custom API Call | any path | Authenticated escape hatch built on `createCustomApiCallAction` |

**New trigger:**

- **New Comment or Work Note** — polls `sys_journal_field` filtered by table and (optionally) record `sys_id`, with `LAST_ITEM` deduplication keyed by journal entry `sys_id`. Filtering happens server-side via the encoded query, so a high-traffic table doesn't drown the engine in discarded rows.

**Internal cleanup that came along for the ride:**
- `@activepieces/piece-service-now` registered in `tsconfig.base.json` (was missing on `main`).
- Extracted a `resolveSysId` helper (4 copies of dropdown-or-manual-id resolution collapsed into one).
- `INCIDENT_STATE` and `JOURNAL_ELEMENT` named constants instead of magic `'6'` / `'7'` / `'comments'` strings.
- Trimmed Zod schemas that only re-validated framework `required: true` props.
- Bumped the piece `0.1.3 → 0.2.0`.

### Relevant User Scenarios

- **L1 helpdesk auto-resolution:** ticket comes in via webhook → AI summarizes → `Search Knowledge Articles` returns top match → `Add Comment` posts the suggested article + link to the incident → if confidence high, `Resolve or Close Incident` closes it with the right close code.
- **Slack-driven ITSM:** Slack message routed through the flow → `Submit Catalog Request` orders the item on behalf of the user → response posts the request number back to Slack.
- **Cross-system sync:** Jira/Linear bug created → `Create Record` opens the matching `problem` record in ServiceNow → on resolution in Jira, `Add Comment` mirrors the resolution back.
- **SLA / on-call notification:** `Count Records` against `incident` filtered by `priority=1^assignment_group={team}` runs on a schedule; if count exceeds threshold, `Send Email` to the on-call group.
- **Audit/closeout automation:** `New Comment or Work Note` trigger filtered to a specific record sys_id watches a high-priority incident; any new internal note pages the owner through Slack/Teams.
- **Onboarding:** new hire flow in HRIS → `Submit Catalog Request` orders laptop, software access, and parking pass with the right `requested_for` user sys_id; response request numbers are stored back in the HR record.

Fixes # (issue)
title: feat(service-now): expand actions and triggers, add knowledge/email/c…

body:
## What does this PR do?

Makes the Azure OpenAI provider's API version configurable in the platform admin panel. Previously, the provider hardcoded `api-version=2024-10-21` for the `/openai/deployments` listing call, which returns `404 Resource not found` on Azure resources that don't accept that version (older Azure OpenAI Service resources, certain regions, and some Azure AI Foundry-provisioned resources). Admins can now set a custom **API Version** on the provider config; the value is used for both deployment listing and inference calls. The field is optional and defaults to `2024-10-21`, so existing Azure providers, connections, and flows continue to work without any change.


### Explain How the Feature Works

A new optional **API Version** field is added to the Azure provider configuration form alongside **Resource Name**:

- Leave it empty → the platform uses the default (`2024-10-21` for the deployments listing; the `@ai-sdk/azure` SDK default for inference). Behaviour is identical to today.
- Set a value (e.g. `2023-03-15-preview`, `2024-02-01`, etc.) → that version is sent on both the listing call (`/openai/deployments?api-version=...`) and on inference requests issued by the AI SDK.

Empty strings are normalised to `undefined` at the schema layer, so saving the field blank is equivalent to not setting it. No database migration is required — the value lives inside the existing JSON config column on the `ai_provider` row.

<!-- [Insert the video link here] -->


### Relevant User Scenarios

- **Klippa (Pylon [#4620](https://app.usepylon.com/support/issues/views/all-issues?issueNumber=4620))** — Cannot configure the Azure provider because their tenant only accepts `2023-03-15-preview` for the deployments endpoint. The hardcoded `2024-10-21` returns 404 against `https://yyy.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments`. With this change they can set the version manually and complete the setup.
- **Customers on Azure AI Foundry / classic Azure OpenAI Service** — The accepted set of API versions on `/openai/deployments` varies by how the resource was provisioned. A configurable version unblocks any tenant whose accepted set doesn't include our default.
- **Regional Azure deployments** — Some Azure regions roll out new API versions later than others; admins in those regions can pin to whatever version their region currently supports without waiting for a platform release.



Fixes Pylon [#4620](https://app.usepylon.com/support/issues/views/all-issues?issueNumber=4620)
title: fix(ai-providers): make Azure API version configurable

body:
## What does this PR do?

Adds a new **Input Images** field to the **Generate Image** action in the AI piece, letting users pass one or more images alongside their prompt for editing, variation, and merge workflows. The field is shown unconditionally below the prompt and is forwarded to whichever image model the user has picked — Nano Banana / Nano Banana 2 / Nano Banana Pro on Google, `gpt-image-1` and `dall-e-2` on OpenAI, and the matching Azure / OpenRouter / Cloudflare Gateway routes.

Previously, image inputs were only available as a hidden, model-specific field nested under **Advanced Options** for `gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview`. This PR promotes it to a first-class top-level input, wires it through both AI SDK code paths (`generateImage` for OpenAI/Azure-style providers, `generateText` for Gemini-style providers), and keeps existing flows working through a runtime backwards-compat fallback.

### Explain How the Feature Works

The action's run handler collects images from the new top-level `inputImages` array and passes them down through one of two paths depending on the resolved provider:

- **OpenAI / Azure / Bedrock** — uses the AI SDK's `generateImage({ prompt: { text, images } })` shape, decoding each `ApFile` into a Buffer before sending. When no images are attached, the prompt stays a plain string so the existing text-to-image behavior is unchanged.
- **Google / Activepieces / OpenRouter / Cloudflare Gateway** — uses `generateText` with `ImagePart`s embedded in the user message. MIME type for the data URL is inferred from the file extension and clamped to a small image-only allowlist (`jpeg`, `png`, `gif`, `webp`, `avif`) with a `image/jpeg` fallback.

A small `stripLegacyImageField` helper drops the legacy `image` key from `advancedOptions` before forwarding to provider-specific options so old saved flows don't leak that field into the provider request body.

### Relevant User Scenarios

- **Photo editing / retouching** — pass an image plus a natural-language instruction ("remove the background", "make the sky sunset orange") to Nano Banana or `gpt-image-1`.
- **Image variations** — generate alternative versions of an uploaded image with `dall-e-2`.
- **Image merging / compositing** — pass two or more images to Nano Banana with a prompt describing how to combine them.
- **Batch image transformation** — drive any of the above from a flow that loops over rows from a table, files from a Drive folder, or attachments from an inbox.
- **Brand-asset workflows** — feed a logo or product shot into the action and ask for placements on different backgrounds, generating a series of marketing variants in one flow.

Fixes # (issue)
title: feat(ai): add input images support for generate image action

`stripSemverRanges` only handles exact versions and `^`/`~` ranges. The plain `"*"` wildcard passed through `resolveWorkspaceDependencies` unchanged (it only resolves `workspace:*` prefixes), causing the throw at `workspace-utils.ts:73`.

body:
## Summary

After deploys, users behind Cloudflare were occasionally seeing **unstyled pages** with the browser logging:

> Refused to apply style from '…/assets/index-XXXX.css' because its MIME type ('application/json') is not a supported stylesheet MIME type.

**Root cause.** `index.html` was served with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=120`, so for up to two minutes after a deploy Cloudflare's edge could keep returning a stale `index.html` whose hashed asset URLs (e.g. `/assets/index-OLDHASH.js`) no longer existed at the origin. The browser then requested those vanished hashes, our `setNotFoundHandler` returned a JSON 404 body, and the browser refused to load JSON as JS/CSS — boom, unstyled page.

**Fix.** Switch the HTML cache header to `no-cache`. Browsers and CDNs may store the file but must revalidate against the origin every time, so stale HTML can never reference dead asset hashes. `fastify-static`'s ETag handling keeps the common case to a cheap 304. Hashed `/assets/*` files keep their 1-year `immutable` cache; all other static files revalidate via `public, max-age=0, must-revalidate`.

## Test plan

- [ ] Deploy two builds back-to-back; confirm the second build's users get the fresh `index.html` immediately (no 2-minute window of broken styles).
- [ ] `curl -I https://<host>/index.html` → `Cache-Control: no-cache`.
- [ ] `curl -I https://<host>/assets/<hashed>.js` → `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` (unchanged).
- [ ] Repeated page loads issue conditional `If-None-Match` requests and receive `304 Not Modified` (verify in devtools network tab).
title: fix(server): always revalidate index.html to avoid stale assets behind Cloudflare
